message:
feat(resolv): port libresolv ns_initparse + ns_parserr + ns_skiprr + ns_msg_getflag (bd-kf793)

Adds the four DNS message parsing primitives that callers of
libresolv expect, alongside the C-compatible `ns_msg` and `ns_rr`
storage types they operate on. Layouts match glibc's
`<arpa/nameser.h>` exactly so an `ns_msg` storage initialized through
this implementation can be passed through to any other libresolv-
shaped code.

New layout / constants:

- `NS_HFIXEDSZ = 12`, `NS_QFIXEDSZ = 4`, `NS_RRFIXEDSZ = 10`,
  `NS_MAXDNAME = 1025`, `NS_S_MAX = 4`.
- `#[repr(C)] CNsMsg` — 80-byte handle: `_msg`, `_eom`, `_id`,
  `_flags`, `_counts[4]`, `_sections[4]`, `_sect`, `_rrnum`,
  `_msg_ptr`.
- `#[repr(C)] CNsRr` — 1048-byte record buffer: `name[1025]`,
  `_type`, `rr_class`, `ttl`, `rdlength`, `rdata`.

Helpers:

- `read_be_u16_at` / `read_be_u32_at` — bounds-checked big-endian
  reads from a `&[u8]` at an offset, returning `Option`.
- `ns_skip_one_rr(msg_buf, eom, pos, section)` — internal helper
  that uses `dn_skipname` to walk past the owner name, then skips
  `NS_QFIXEDSZ` (qd) or `NS_RRFIXEDSZ + rdlength` (an/ns/ar) per
  the section.

Public API:

- `ns_initparse(msg, msglen, *handle) -> int`: validates `msglen >=
  NS_HFIXEDSZ`, decodes id/flags/counts from the header, walks each
  of the four sections to find boundaries (writing into
  `_sections[4]`), then stores the populated handle. Returns 0 on
  success, -1 on malformed input.
- `ns_parserr(*handle, section, rrnum, *rr) -> int`: walks from the
  section's start to the requested `rrnum`-th RR each call (no
  cursor caching — matches the historical glibc semantics callers
  rely on) and fills the [`CNsRr`] storage with type/class/ttl/
  rdlength/rdata. Returns 0 on success, -1 on bounds error or
  malformed message. The handle's `_sect` and `_rrnum` are updated
  to reflect the last accessed RR, mirroring glibc.
- `ns_skiprr(ptr, eom, section, count) -> int`: skips `count` RRs
  starting at `ptr` and returns the bytes consumed. Returns -1 on
  malformed input or bounds violation. Section 0 (qd) skips
  `name + NS_QFIXEDSZ`; sections 1..=3 skip `name + NS_RRFIXEDSZ +
  rdlength`.
- `ns_msg_getflag(handle, flag) -> int`: extracts a header flag bit
  from a parsed handle per RFC 1035 — QR (bit 15), opcode
  (bits 11..=14), AA/TC/RD/RA (bits 10..=7), Z/AD/CD (bits 6..=4),
  RCODE (bits 0..=3). Returns the extracted value, or 0 for
  unknown flag indices / NULL handle (matches glibc's silent
  treat-as-zero behavior).

Existing dn_expand / dn_skipname helpers in `unistd_abi` are reused
for owner-name decoding so this PORT does not duplicate name-parser
logic.

Closes the implementation half of bd-kf793; closing the bead and
adding tests will follow in subsequent commits per the standard
res_* PORT cadence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
